blurb: The homepage of Civic Thriving, an initiative focused on strengthening civic health and community wellbeing -- included here as a resource hub rather than a single article.
ambiguous_note: Site content beyond the homepage isn't confirmable from the title/URL alone.

title_clean: Your Imagination Was Always Empire's Last Frontier
blurb: A Substack essay arguing that colonial power has always worked by capturing and narrowing what people can imagine as possible, making collective imagination itself a site of liberation.
A hub for postnormal times theory -- Ziauddin Sardar's framework for thinking through an era defined by complexity, chaos, and contradictions where old certainties no longer hold.
A Rithm Project report examining how young people's relationships with AI tools and companions are shaping their social development and sense of connection.
ambiguous_note: Hosted as a Google Drive PDF; full contents not independently verified beyond the title.

blurb: The 52nd edition of Harvard's long-running youth poll, tracking young Americans' political attitudes, trust in institutions, and outlook on the future.

title_clean: The Futurism You Were Sold Was a Weapon
blurb: Futurist Monika Bielskyte's critique of corporate and techno-utopian futurism, arguing that mainstream visions of the future have been weaponized to serve extraction and control rather than genuine possibility.

A report applying lessons from the UK's Big Local community-power initiative to the idea of 'civic rewilding' -- letting local civic life regenerate its own structures rather than importing them from outside.
James Plunkett's Medium essay on how large institutions and systems lose vitality at their center even as innovation and energy persist at their margins.
A video essay drawing a line from football stadium chants to techno club rituals, exploring why shared, embodied ritual still generates belonging in a fragmented culture.
Bayo Akomolafe's essay reframing urgency itself as a colonial reflex, arguing that some of the most transformative responses to crisis require slowing down rather than rushing toward solutions.
A Substack essay on the tension between Gen Z's strong stated moral commitments and the pressures of a culture and economy that make those commitments hard to live out consistently.

title_clean: New Poll: Mental Health Challenges Prevalent Among Gen Z Youth
blurb: A Blue Shield of California poll finding that more than three in four Gen Z youth have discussed their mental health struggles with someone else, underscoring both prevalence and growing openness.
A United Way of the Bay Area survey on how Gen Z engages with activism and civic participation -- what motivates them, what discourages them, and where they see themselves making change.
Deloitte's annual global survey of Gen Z and millennial attitudes toward work, money, climate, and mental health, tracked year over year across dozens of countries.
The workshop offerings page for generational historian Eliza Filby, who advises organizations on navigating generational change and intergenerational tension in the workplace.
ambiguous_note: This is a services/workshop listing page rather than a single piece of content; specific workshop content isn't confirmable from the URL alone.

blurb: Artefact's Tarot Cards of Tech is a foresight card deck and framework designed to help teams surface the unintended consequences of a new technology before they build it.
A widely-used open-source library of facilitation microstructures -- alternatives to standard meetings and brainstorms designed to include everyone and unleash a group's collective intelligence.
A 2026 report on what makes experiences memorable and meaningful, examined here for what it suggests about designing moments of wonder and connection rather than routine ones.
ambiguous_note: Full authorship and methodology of this report aren't confirmable from the title and file path alone.

blurb: A Harvard Business Review piece arguing that how empathetically leaders roll out AI tools -- not just the tools themselves -- determines whether employees trust and adopt them.
A Medium essay on how the IMAGINE initiative is trying to give a fragmented field a shared framework and common language for its work.
ambiguous_note: What field and what IMAGINE refers to specifically aren't confirmable from the title alone.

blurb: An SF Chronicle opinion piece reporting on conversations with 100 young people about how they see AI shaping their futures -- their hopes, anxieties, and expectations.
A paper examining whether and how strategic foresight work can be treated as legitimate evidence within policymaking processes that are usually built around retrospective data.
ambiguous_note: Hosted as a shared LinkedIn document; full text not independently verified beyond the title.

blurb: An academic paper presenting a framework for classifying media content according to the models of masculinity it portrays, developed for use in gender-focused media research.
ambiguous_note: Hosted as a Google Drive document; full methodology not independently verified beyond the title.

blurb: A Springer journal article that maps futures literacy -- the capacity to imagine and use the future -- as a structured space of competencies, building on UNESCO's futures literacy framework.
